ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 covers onion ring as a food product; jumbo sizing is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated jumbo onion ring entry. A00LC (battered fried onion rings) is the closest defensible parent code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for onion rings as a food product. SNOMED CT does not routinely code prepared snack foods at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Jumbo Onion Rings is approximately 120 g, equivalent to 6–8 large battered and fried onion rings as typically served at a fast-food or casual dining outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Onion rings, breaded, par fried, frozen, prepared, heated in oven (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 35188)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for breaded, par-fried, oven-heated onion rings used as the closest approved reference. Jumbo onion rings from Potato Corner are deep-fried with a seasoned coating; values are adapted from the USDA reference and cross-checked against USDA Branded Food entries for comparable battered fried onion ring products. Energy and macros are consistent with deep-fried battered onion ring profiles reported in USDA data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for battered fried onion rings
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for jumbo onion rings specifically. GI of 76 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 Atkinson et al. international GI tables (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which report GI values for battered and fried potato-based and wheat-battered snack foods in the range of 70–80. Battered fried onion rings share a similar carbohydrate matrix (refined wheat flour coating, high-temperature frying) with these reference foods. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 40.8) / 100 ≈ 31.0, using 120 g serving with 34.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (390 mg per 100 g; approximately 468 mg per 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid jumbo onion rings due to their high sodium content, which can contribute meaningfully to the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg sodium; a single serving provides approximately 23% of this limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 76) and high glycemic load per serving (GL ~31), driven by refined wheat flour batter and deep-frying
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit jumbo onion rings, as the high GI and high GL per serving can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; the refined carbohydrate batter and deep-frying process further reduce the food's suitability for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (14 g per 100 g), saturated fat (2.1 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.1 g per 100 g), and high sodium, combined with deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of jumbo onion rings due to their combination of high total fat, saturated fat, trace trans fat, and high sodium, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jumbo Onion Rings are large, battered, and deep-fried onion rings made from thick-cut onion slices coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and fried until golden and crispy. The exterior is light and crunchy with a savoury, well-seasoned flavour, while the interior onion becomes soft and mildly sweet from the heat. In Singapore, they are served as a popular snack or side dish, notably at Potato Corner outlets, where they may be paired with flavoured seasoning powders such as truffle, barbecue, or cheese. Nutritionally, jumbo onion rings are energy-dense at approximately 276 kcal per 100 g, with a significant carbohydrate load (34 g per 100 g) from the refined wheat flour batter, moderate fat content (14 g per 100 g) from deep-frying in vegetable oil, and moderate sodium (390 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is high (estimated GI 76), making them unsuitable for individuals managing blood glucose. The high sodium content raises concern for those with hypertension, and the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium is relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. They are vegetarian and dairy-free but contain gluten. A typical serving of 6–8 jumbo rings weighs approximately 120 g and delivers around 31 glycemic load units, classifying this as a high-GL snack food.
common_user_queries: How many calories in jumbo onion rings?
Are onion rings high in sodium?
What is the GI of onion rings?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data exists for Potato Corner Singapore's Jumbo Onion Rings in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded, par-fried, oven-heated onion rings and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable deep-fried battered onion ring products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA reference is a strong structural match and the adaptation is well-documented.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 35188 / FDC ID 172950) for breaded, par-fried, oven-heated onion rings, adjusted upward slightly for energy and fat to reflect deep-frying rather than oven heating, consistent with USDA Branded Food comparators. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 3. Vitamin B12 is reported as 0.0 in the USDA source for plant-based battered snack foods with no animal-derived ingredients; this is a confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. Cholesterol is reported as 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is a confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product type, consistent with the absence of egg or dairy in the standard batter formulation. 5. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for jumbo onion rings was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Halal status is assumed based on the absence of pork or alcohol ingredients in the standard product; formal halal certification status for Potato Corner Singapore should be verified independently. 7. Kosher status is not confirmed. 8. The food is classified as Western / Fast Food cuisine; multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the serving context is Singapore.
